resources.string: Mounts remote SSH/SFTP servers as file system roots accessible from any Android app that uses the Storage Access Framework (SAF) file picker. Private keys and configuration are stored encrypted using the Android Keystore so they never leave the device in plain text.
Import an SSH config bundle and manage which servers are available as storage locations on this device.

📦 Import a .tgz bundle containing your SSH config file and private keys. Once imported, your SSH servers appear as storage locations in the Android file picker and any app that supports the Storage Access Framework.
❌ Archive must contain a file named \'config\'.
❌ No valid Host entries found in the SSH config.
📭 Nothing to export — no configuration imported yet.

SSH FS Provider mounts remote SSH/SFTP servers as document roots inside the Android Storage Access Framework (SAF). Once configured, any app that uses the system file picker — Files, photo pickers, text editors, video players — can open, save, and browse files directly on your servers without needing a separate sync step.

Create a .tgz archive (tar + gzip) containing the following files in a flat structure (no sub-directories):

 • config — OpenSSH-format config file (required)
 • known_hosts — host key verification file (recommended)
 • <keyfile> — one or more private key files (e.g. id_ed25519)

 Example archive structure:

 bundle.tgz
 ├── config
 ├── known_hosts
 └── id_ed25519

 Example config file:

 Host myserver
   HostName 192.168.1.10
   User alice
   Port 22
   IdentityFile id_ed25519

 Create the bundle on your computer:

 tar -czf bundle.tgz -C ~/.ssh config known_hosts id_ed25519

1. Transfer bundle.tgz to your Android device (email, USB, cloud storage, etc.).
 2. Open SSH FS Provider and tap Import config bundle (.tgz).
 3. Select the bundle file in the file picker.
 4. A success message confirms how many hosts and keys were imported.

 The app reads the IdentityFile value from each Host block and looks for a matching filename inside the archive. Only the filename is used — paths are stripped.
🖥️ Using SSH servers in other apps

After a successful import, SSH servers appear as storage roots in the Android file picker:

 • Open any app that supports file access (e.g. Files by Google, a text editor, a video player).
 • Tap Open or Browse, then look for your server name in the left drawer or the list of storage locations.
 • Navigate directories and open files just like local storage.
 • Writes are buffered locally and uploaded atomically when the file is closed, so the remote file is never left in a partially-written state.

Including a known_hosts file in your bundle is strongly recommended. When present, SSH FS Provider verifies the server's host key against the file before connecting (StrictHostKeyChecking = yes). Without it, host key checking is disabled and the connection is vulnerable to man-in-the-middle attacks.

 Generate known_hosts for a specific server:

 ssh-keyscan -H myserver.example.com >> ~/.ssh/known_hosts

• Private keys and the config file are stored encrypted with AES-256-GCM using a master key held in the Android Keystore — they cannot be extracted without the device unlock credential.
 • Android Backup is disabled so keys are never uploaded to Google's backup servers.
 • Key bytes are zeroed in memory immediately after they are handed to the SSH library.
 • The Documents Provider is accessible only through the system document picker; third-party apps cannot query it directly.

Key types: Ed25519, ECDSA (nistp256), RSA (rsa-sha2-256 / rsa-sha2-512)
 KEX: curve25519-sha256, ecdh-sha2-nistp256,
             diffie-hellman-group14-sha256
 Auth: Public-key only
 Operations: Browse, open, create, write, delete, rename
 Large files: Seek-capable reads via server-side offset; writes buffered locally then uploaded atomically

Server not appearing in file picker
 • Re-import the bundle — the file picker refreshes automatically.
 • Check that the config file contains at least one Host block.

 Connection refused / timeout
 • Verify hostname, port, and that the server is reachable from the device network.

 Authentication failed
 • Confirm the key file name in IdentityFile exactly matches the filename in the bundle.
 • Ensure the corresponding public key is in ~/.ssh/authorized_keys on the server.

 Host key verification failed
 • Re-run ssh-keyscan and re-import with the updated known_hosts.
